problem_addressed: Open-set active learning requires effective handling of unknown classes and stable high-precision queries, especially in early training stages where existing methods suffer from instability and suboptimal performance.
core_claim: The proposed method achieves superior query precision and test accuracy by dynamically estimating unknown classes, regulating purity via GMM, and using information-based selection, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines across benchmarks.
key_contributions: A purity-based candidate selection framework using auxiliary head outputs and GMM-modeled purity regimes (high/known, low/unknown, intermediate/ambiguous).
A flexible sampling strategy that adapts to unknown class estimates and maintains high query precision from early rounds without additional hyperparameters.
novelty: Introduces dynamic unknown class estimation via Algorithm 1, GMM-based purity regime modeling for stability, and a two-phase refinement (precision-based then information-based) using JS divergence scores.
method_summary: Trains a dual-head classifier (LCE for known classes, LEDL for unknown), computes purity margins using auxiliary head logits, fits a 3-component GMM to logit margins, refines candidates via calibrated precision targets, and selects queries using JS divergence scores.
method_components: Purity margin computation: Spurity(x) = maxc∈Ck oc (known) or maxc∈Ck oc − maxc∈Cˆu oc (unknown).
GMM fitting on logit margins to model purity regimes and likelihood-based sorting.
Precision-based refinement using calibrated target precision ˆp∗t.
Information-based selection via JS divergence: Sinfo(x) = JS(p∥u) · JS(p∥pmax).
key_innovation: Dynamic purity regulation through GMM-modeled regimes and adaptive unknown estimation enables stable high-precision queries without manual hyperparameter tuning.
assumptions: Access to labeled pool DL and unlabeled pool DU.
Upper bound ˆumax for unknown class count estimation.
Availability of Algorithm 1 for unknown estimation.
theoretical_grounding: Empirical validation via cross-round precision/accuracy curves and comparative analysis against baselines; no explicit theoretical proofs provided.
hyperparameters: ResNet-18 for CIFAR datasets, ResNet-50 for ImageNet; 10-fold cross-validation; budget of 1000 samples for CIFAR datasets and 2000 for ImageNet
improvement_over_baseline: OSAL improves over Open-Set BALD by 1.9% on CIFAR-10, 1.7% on CIFAR-100, 2.2% on Tiny-ImageNet, and 1.8% on ImageNet-1K
title: Revisiting Unknowns: Towards Effective and Efficient Open-Set Active Learning
